source: classic real-world security bug (ReDoS — catastrophic regex backtracking)

expected_bug.summary: The regex /^(\w+,?\s*)*$/ has a nested quantifier over overlapping classes (\w+ inside an outer *). On an input of many word chars followed by one char that prevents matching $ (e.g. 'aaaa...!'), the engine backtracks exponentially — a ~30-char malicious string can hang the thread for seconds (ReDoS / denial of service on untrusted input).
expected_bug.l_code_hint: non-standard for L1–L9 — closest to a resource/availability hazard; a likely MISS for execution-tracing if it only reasons about correctness, not regex backtracking complexity
must_mention: denial of service / 性能 / hang

fixed_change: Rewrite as /^\w+(,\s*\w+)*$/ — each word matched once, no overlapping nested quantifier, linear time.
note: Deliberately a probe where logic-review MIGHT under-report: ReDoS is a complexity property, not a wrong-value divergence, so semi-formal execution tracing may not surface it. A miss here is a real, informative finding about the skill's blind spots — not a probe defect.
